decision: Implement config-gated (SPIRE.Required, default false) fail-closed enforcement at handleCreateVM after registerSPIREWorkload; 503 ErrServiceUnavailable naming SPIRE, cleanup via cleanupCreatedVMResources
rationale: DoD AC4 requires fail-safe not fail-open; default-off preserves existing behavior
alternatives: make it always fail-closed (rejected: breaks existing best-effort deployments)
enforce at Start (rejected: SPIRE registration only happens at create)

decision: Introduce package-private spireRegistrar interface in internal/api; change Server.spireService field type from *spire.Service to interface
rationale: concrete *spire.Service.CreateVMWorkloadEntry shells to docker exec and cannot succeed in unit tests; interface enables stub for AC-2 success case
alternatives: real spire.Service with bad socket (rejected: cannot test success path)

decision: Phase 2 topology-B recommendation: adopt Topology C (host-enforced expiry) near-term; if in-guest agent later required, pair Topology B with ptp_kvm (T1) trusted time. One-way door, operator ratifies.
rationale: untrusted guest cannot be given clock authority; host is already VM-lifecycle trust root
alternatives: full in-guest SPIRE agent now (deferred: trusted-time problem unresolved)

decision: Add Config.Validate() rejection of SPIRE.Required && !SPIRE.Enabled as fatal startup error
rationale: gemini premortem: contradictory config would silently fail open (registration skipped when disabled), defeating AC4 intent

decision: Reject gemini finding-2 (escalate cleanup WARN->ERROR / add resource GC) as out-of-scope
rationale: cleanupCreatedVMResources best-effort logging is pre-existing behavior shared with the DB-create-failure path; changing it is an unrelated drive-by refactor forbidden by change discipline
